sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: Analysis of the supplied May 2026 pharmaceutical news articles reveals no explicit data regarding Proteolysis Targeting Chimeras (PROTAC) technology or related pipeline developments. Consequently, this intelligence brief synthesizes the available industry context, which centers on regulatory and scientific stability. Jeremy Levin of Ovid Therapeutics highlights risks to American biotech leadership due to institutional distrust and regulatory upheaval. Policy discussions focus on the Trump administration testing Most Favored Nation (MFN) drug pricing deals, impacting market access strategies. Scientific integrity concerns are raised by the SCORE project’s replication crisis findings, relevant to clinical trial reliability. Additionally, Eli Lilly reported early data for Verve Therapeutics’ gene editor for high cholesterol, illustrating progress in genetic medicine modalities. While these developments define the current landscape, the absence of PROTAC-specific reporting in the source material limits targeted analysis on targeted protein degradation technologies. Stakeholders should monitor future releases for specific PROTAC clinical or commercial updates.

points.text: Ovid Therapeutics — Jeremy Levin — Industry Leadership — Warns of institutional distrust and regulatory risk to biotech future.
White House — MFN Deals — Drug Pricing — Trump administration deals set to be tested by new product launches.
SCORE Project — Replication Study — Social Science — Verdict on 4,000 papers shows roughly half did not hold up.
Eli Lilly — Verve Gene Editor — High Cholesterol — Early study data shows promise for cholesterol lowering.
Ovid Therapeutics: Jeremy Levin (Founder/Chairman) advocates for federal changes to incentivize investment.
Teva Pharmaceuticals: Jeremy Levin (Former CEO) cited regarding industry leadership risks.
Eli Lilly: Strategic investment in gene editing via Verve Therapeutics partnership.
Verve Therapeutics: Developing gene editor for high cholesterol, supported by Eli Lilly.
Verve Gene Editor: Early study data indicates lowering of cholesterol levels; mechanism involves gene editing.
SCORE Project: Direct replication of nearly 4,000 social-science papers found roughly half did not hold up.
Trump Administration MFN Deals: Drug pricing policies set to be tested by new product launches.
Eli Lilly/Verve: Partnership focused on gene editor technology for high cholesterol treatment.
